content:
Why Remote Workers Need a VPN

When you work from a coffee shop, hotel, or home network, your traffic passes through infrastructure you did not set up and cannot audit. A VPN encrypts your connection between your device and the VPN server, so anyone intercepting your traffic at the cafe sees only encrypted noise. Your employer's IT team and your ISP both benefit from this added layer.

What a VPN Actually Protects

A VPN protects data in transit between your device and the VPN server. It prevents network-level interception on the local network you are using. It hides your traffic content from your ISP. It masks your IP address from the websites and services you visit.

A VPN does not protect you from phishing, malware on your own device, or data breaches at the services you use. It is one layer of security, not a complete solution.

Best VPNs for Remote Work in 2026

NordVPN and ExpressVPN remain the most reliable choices for individual remote workers. Both have fast speeds, kill switches that cut your connection if the VPN drops, and no-logs policies that have been independently audited. Mullvad is the best option if privacy is the primary concern: they accept cash payments and require no account email at signup.

For teams, NordLayer (NordVPN's business product) and Perimeter 81 offer centralized management, device policies, and integration with identity providers. These are worth the extra cost for teams of five or more.

Configuration Tips

Enable the kill switch in your VPN app. Without it, a dropped VPN connection silently falls back to your unencrypted local network. Turn on auto-connect on untrusted networks. Use split tunneling to keep local printer and file-sharing traffic off the VPN while routing everything else through it. On corporate networks, check with your IT team first, as some organizations route VPN traffic through their own gateways.
description: Working from home or a cafe means your data travels over networks you do not control. Here is what a VPN does and does not protect, and which ones to use.
title: VPN for Remote Work: A Security Guide for 2026
